title: ADK-302: Intent Classification & Routing Agents
description: Configure router agents to classify semantic intents and dispatch task execution parameters to worker sub-agents.

highlights.title: Supervisor Design
highlights.description: Design a centralized supervisor agent that processes input queries and selects downstream executors.

highlights.title: Intent Parsing
highlights.description: Apply classification prompts or vector similarities to tag messages with specialized labels.

highlights.title: Fallback Paths
highlights.description: Handle complex and unresolvable intents with grace, escalating queries to manual intervention channels.

blocks.content: Supervisor-Worker Design Pattern

For large projects, a single agent with 50 tools will make errors. The Supervisor-Worker pattern breaks this down. A supervisor agent handles triage, extracting intent and delegating sub-tasks to specialized sub-agents with 3-5 tools each.

blocks.title: Architecture
blocks.content: The supervisor agent is the primary endpoint, and it routes payloads to sub-agents via the A2A network protocol. It consolidates the final responses.
Check Your Understanding

Question 1: What is the main benefit of the Supervisor-Worker pattern?
- A) It removes the need for LLMs.
- B) It prevents tool selection confusion by isolating tools within small, focused sub-agents.
- C) It is faster to execute.

Feedback: Option B is correct. Focus limits selection mistakes.

modules.title: 2. Building intent classifiers using semantic embedding

blocks.content: Intent Classification via Embeddings
Before invoking the agent loop, comparing the cosine similarity of the user's query against a dataset of labeled intent vectors allows choosing the best agent configuration quickly.
Classification Python Mock
Check Your Understanding

Question 1: Why use intent classification prior to triggering model execution?
- A) It encrypts the user's input.
- B) It skips querying the expensive reasoning LLM for simple path routing.
- C) It compiles custom tool modules.

Feedback: Option B is correct. Pre-routing limits token costs.

modules.title: 3. Routing messages dynamically to specialized sub-agents

blocks.content: Dynamic Multi-Agent Routing
Once the supervisor identifies the target worker agent, it formats the task payload and sends it using the A2AServer routing system.
A2A Dynamic Dispatch Code

blocks.code:
from google_adk.a2a import A2AServer

async def route_task(target_agent: str, task_desc: str):
    server = A2AServer()
    # Send request to sub-agent worker channel
    response = await server.send_message(
        recipient=target_agent,
        message=task_desc
    )
    return response

blocks.content: Check Your Understanding

Question 1: What protocol facilitates programmatic dispatching between agents?
- A) HTTP OAuth2
- B) A2A (Agent-to-Agent)
- C) TCP Socket

Feedback: Option B is correct. A2A handles multi-agent messages.

modules.title: 4. Handling escalation pathways and fallback policies

blocks.content: Escalation & Fallbacks

When an agent fails to match a route, or the sub-agent returns an execution failure, the supervisor must execute a fallback policy. This usually means escalating to a human operator or returning a friendly message.

blocks.title: Loop Detection
blocks.content: If two agents continually refer queries to each other, they will create an execution loop. Implement a 'max_redirects' header in agent messages to break loops.
Check Your Understanding

Question 1: How do you prevent infinite redirection loops in multi-agent routing?
- A) Stop executing code.
- B) Track a redirection counter (e.g. max_redirects) in the message envelope.
- C) Enforce database transactions.

Feedback: Option B is correct. Counters prevent loops.

modules.title: 5. Lab: Designing a triage routing agent

blocks.content: Lab: Designing a Triage Routing Agent
In this lab, you will write a complete triage routing program in Python that reads queries, classifies them, and dispatches messages to the appropriate agent worker.
Step 1: Write the routing logic
Check Your Understanding

Question 1: Which worker handled the invoice query in the script?
- A) Support Worker
- B) Billing Worker
- C) None

Feedback: Option B is correct. Billing Worker matched the 'invoice' keyword.
